source.section: 2 - AN/APG-73 Radar > 2.1 - Air-to-Air Modes > 2.1.6 - Radar Spotlight Option

steps.action: Position the TDC over the area you wish to spotlight
steps.voiced: Slew TDC over the area you want to spotlight.

steps.action: Press and hold the TDC for more than one second.
steps.voiced: Press and hold TDC for more than one second.

steps.action: The radar azimuth indicator will drive to the TDC location and begin a scan centered on the cursor and constrained to 22° of azimuth.
steps.voiced: Radar drives to that position and begins a 22-degree azimuth scan centered on the cursor.

steps.action: The scan area can be slewed by pressing and holding the TDC while moving it left or right.
steps.voiced: Press and hold TDC while slewing left or right to reposition the scan area.

steps.action: To exit Spotlight, depress and release the TDC. Alternatively, press Undesignate or assign TDC priority to another display. (Note: SPOT sub-mode can also be used with an AACQ mode. When SPOT sub-mode is) activated from an AACQ mode, the radar will attempt to STT the first target detected while SPOT is active.
steps.voiced: Depress and release TDC to exit Spotlight, or press Undesignate Button, or reassign TDC priority.
